---
title: 'Chocolate Lab Faith'
date: '2011-10-19T04:25:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: '(My Journal) Fed By Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '6072541567489371454'
blogger_filename: '/2011/10/chocolate-lab-faith.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/(My Journal) Fed By Faith/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Life Events'
  - 'Answers'
---

My wife and I tried to have dogs when we first moved into our house. This proved to be too much new at one time. So as our year approached she went to praying about having a dog. She and the boys really wanted one. I could take it or leave it but I enjoy animals of many kinds and it's their hearts desire. I loved having dogs growing up. So she puts in her prayer book that the right dog, comes at the right time, and we are asked to take it. No shopping, no looking, no telling anyone we are "In the market" so to speak. In fact she doesn't even tell me beyond once or twice in passing. She also adds that she has always wanted a lab (especially a chocolate lab).<br />
<br />
Fast&nbsp;forward. Yesterday she gets a call from someone at the church who got our number from someone else. She has a newborn and needs someone to take a dog off their hands. It's a 9 month old chocolate lab. Fully vaccinated with her own kennel. Potty Trained and kennel trained. It took about 30 seconds to pray about that one since it's exactly the line up she's been believing for.<br />
<br />
<a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/p1030579-9272e9ba0bb6.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="clear: left; float: left; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" height="240" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/p1030579-df78796ed83d.jpg" width="320" /></a>So now we have a<a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Labrador_Retriever"> Chocolate Lab</a>. VERY cool dog. Smarter than any dog I've owned. A bit hyper in the first few hours but calmed down almost immediately after employing some of <a href="http://www.cesarsway.com/">Cesar Millan's</a> methods. She is quite obedient already, even though she is only 9 Months. I'm grateful for the training she&nbsp;received&nbsp;from previous owners. I'm very happy to have her here and look forward to working with her to bring out her best. She is known as Lacey, but we are praying about whether that is the name God intends for her.<br />
<br />
Thank you Jesus for this Random answer to a specific prayer... and for teaching me to pray more specifically in the future.<br />
<br />
&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;Darrell G. Wolfe<br />
<br />
<span class="Apple-style-span" style="background-color: white; font-family: sans-serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">The&nbsp;<b><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Labrador_Retriever">Labrador Retriever</a></b>&nbsp;(also&nbsp;<b>Labrador</b>, or&nbsp;<b>Lab</b>&nbsp;for short) is one of several kinds of&nbsp;<a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Retriever" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #0645ad; text-decoration: none;" title="Retriever">retriever</a>, a type of&nbsp;<a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gun_dog" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #0645ad; text-decoration: none;" title="Gun dog">gun dog</a>. A breed characteristic is webbed paws for swimming, useful for the breed's original purpose of retrieving fishing nets. The Labrador is the most&nbsp;<a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_most_popular_dog_breeds" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #0645ad; text-decoration: none;" title="List of most popular dog breeds">popular</a>&nbsp;<a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dog_breed" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #0645ad; text-decoration: none;" title="Dog breed">breed</a>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dog" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #0645ad; text-decoration: none;" title="Dog">dog</a>&nbsp;by registered ownership in Canada,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-3" style="font-style: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Labrador_Retriever#cite_note-3" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #0645ad; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;">[4]</a></sup>&nbsp;the United Kingdom,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-4" style="font-style: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Labrador_Retriever#cite_note-4" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #0645ad; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;">[5]</a></sup>&nbsp;and the United States (since 1991).<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-5" style="font-style: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Labrador_Retriever#cite_note-5" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #0645ad; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;">[6]</a></sup>&nbsp;It is also one of the most popular&nbsp;<a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assistance_dog" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #0645ad; text-decoration: none;" title="Assistance dog">assistance dog</a>&nbsp;breeds in Australia, Canada, the United Kingdom and the United States and many other countries, as well as being widely used by police and other official bodies for their detection and working abilities. Typically, Labradors are athletic and love to swim, play catch and retrieve games, are good with young children, elderly, and for protection.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-salabclub_6-0" style="font-style: normal; line-height: 1em;"><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Labrador_Retriever#cite_note-salabclub-6" style="background-attachment: initial; background-clip: initial; background-color: initial; background-image: none; background-origin: initial; background-position: initial initial; background-repeat: initial initial; color: #0645ad; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap;">[7]</a></sup></span>
